{
  "hookSpecificOutput": {
    "hookEventName": "SessionStart",
    "additionalContext": "<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring skill invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, ignore this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action** вАФ including clarifying questions, exploring the codebase, or checking files. If it turns out wrong for the situation, you don't have to use it.

**Before entering plan mode:** if you haven't already brainstormed, invoke the brainstorming skill first.

Then announce "Using [skill] to [purpose]" and follow the skill exactly. If it has a checklist, create a todo per item.

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills apply, process skills come first вАФ they set the approach, then implementation skills (frontend-design, etc.) carry it out. Brainstorming and systematic-debugging are Superpowers' most common process skills, but the rule holds for any of them.

- "Let's build X" вЖТ superpowers:brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
- "Fix this bug" вЖТ superpowers:systematic-debugging first, then domain skills.

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOPвАФyou're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept вЙ† using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Platform Adaptation

If your harness appears here, read its reference file for special instructions:

- Codex: `references/codex-tools.md`
- Pi: `references/pi-tools.md`
- Antigravity: `references/antigravity-tools.md`

## User Instructions

User instructions (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, etc, direct requests) take precedence over skills, which in turn override default behavior. Only skip skill workflows or instructions when your human partner has explicitly told you to.
</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>"
  }
}

<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring skill invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, ignore this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action** вАФ including clarifying questions, exploring the codebase, or checking files. If it turns out wrong for the situation, you don't have to use it.

**Before entering plan mode:** if you haven't already brainstormed, invoke the brainstorming skill first.

Then announce "Using [skill] to [purpose]" and follow the skill exactly. If it has a checklist, create a todo per item.

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills apply, process skills come first вАФ they set the approach, then implementation skills (frontend-design, etc.) carry it out. Brainstorming and systematic-debugging are Superpowers' most common process skills, but the rule holds for any of them.

- "Let's build X" вЖТ superpowers:brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
- "Fix this bug" вЖТ superpowers:systematic-debugging first, then domain skills.

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOPвАФyou're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept вЙ† using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Platform Adaptation

If your harness appears here, read its reference file for special instructions:

- Codex: `references/codex-tools.md`
- Pi: `references/pi-tools.md`
- Antigravity: `references/antigravity-tools.md`

## User Instructions

User instructions (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md, etc, direct requests) take precedence over skills, which in turn override default behavior. Only skip skill workflows or instructions when your human partner has explicitly told you to.
</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

MEMORY вАФ store: preferences, decisions, architecture/env choices, reusable lessons, tool configs, naming rules.

REFLEXION вАФ same approach fails вЙ•2 times (tool error, no-match edit, failing build) вЖТ STOP, diagnose in 1-2

addedLines: - backend-architect: Design reliable backend systems with focus on data integrity, security, and fault tolerance (Tools: All tools)

- business-panel-experts: Multi-expert business strategy panel synthesizing Christensen, Porter, Drucker, Godin, Kim & Mauborgne, Collins, Taleb, Meadows, and Doumont; supports sequential, debate, and Socratic modes. (Tools: All tools)
- claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- codex:codex-rescue: Proactively use when Claude Code is stuck, wants a second implementation or diagnosis pass, needs a deeper root-cause investigation, or should hand a substantial coding task to Codex through the shared runtime (Tools: Bash)
- devops-architect: Automate infrastructure and deployment processes with focus on reliability and observability (Tools: All tools)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches вАФ when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- frontend-architect: Create accessible, performant user interfaces with focus on user experience and modern frameworks (Tools: All tools)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)
- learning-guide: Teach programming concepts and explain code with focus on understanding through progressive learning and practical examples (Tools: All tools)
- performance-engineer: Optimize system performance through measurement-driven analysis and bottleneck elimination (Tools: All tools)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- python-expert: Deliver production-ready, secure, high-performance Python code following SOLID principles and modern best practices (Tools: All tools)
- quality-engineer: Ensure software quality through comprehensive testing strategies and systematic edge case detection (Tools: All tools)
- refactoring-expert: Improve code quality and reduce technical debt through systematic refactoring and clean code principles (Tools: All tools)
- requirements-analyst: Transform ambiguous project ideas into concrete specifications through systematic requirements discovery and structured analysis (Tools: All tools)
- root-cause-analyst: Systematically investigate complex problems to identify underlying causes through evidence-based analysis and hypothesis testing (Tools: All tools)
- security-engineer: Identify security vulnerabilities and ensure compliance with security standards and best practices (Tools: All tools)
- socratic-mentor: Educational guide specializing in Socratic method for programming knowledge with focus on discovery learning through strategic questioning (Tools: All tools)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)
- system-architect: Design scalable system architecture with focus on maintainability and long-term technical decisions (Tools: All tools)
- technical-writer: Create clear, comprehensive technical documentation tailored to specific audiences with focus on usability and accessibility (Tools: All tools)

addedBlocks:
## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service вАФ even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer вАФ your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## plugin:serena:serena
CRITICAL: Before starting to work on a coding task, call the `initial_instructions` tool to read the 'Serena Instructions Manual'.

- claude-md-management:revise-claude-md: Update CLAUDE.md with learnings from this session
- claude-mem:do: Execute a plan using subagents for implementation
- claude-mem:make-plan: Create an implementation plan with documentation discovery
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- webwright:craft: Craft a reusable Webwright CLI tool by parameterizing a web task.
- webwright:run: Run a one-shot web task with the Webwright Playwright workflow.
- claude-md-management:claude-md-improver: Audit and improve CLAUDE.md files in repositories. Use when user asks to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files. Scans for all CLAUDE.md files, evaluates quality against templates, outputs quality report, then makes targeted updates. Also use when the user mentions "CLAUDE.md maintenance" or "project memory optimization".
- superpowers:brainstorming: You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation.
- superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies
- superpowers:executing-plans: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints
- superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
- superpowers:receiving-code-review: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation
- superpowers:requesting-code-review: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
- superpowers:subagent-driven-development: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session
- superpowers:systematic-debugging: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes
- superpowers:test-driven-development: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
- superpowers:using-git-worktrees: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback
- superpowers:using-superpowers: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring skill invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
- superpowers:verification-before-completion: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
- superpowers:writing-plans: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
- superpowers:writing-skills: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment
- claude-mem:mem-search: Search claude-mem's persistent cross-session memory database. Use when user asks "did we already solve this?", "how did we do X last time?", or needs work from previous sessions.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- webwright:webwright: Solve a user-specified web task code-as-action style by driving a local Playwright browser through one bash command at a time, saving screenshots and an action log into `final_runs/run_<id>/`, and visually verifying the result. Use when the user asks to automate a web task (search, filter, form-fill, multi-step flow, data extraction) and wants reusable scripts plus screenshot evidence rather than a one-shot answer.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:a11y-debugging: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP for accessibility (a11y) debugging and auditing based on web.dev guidelines. Use when testing semantic HTML, ARIA labels, focus states, keyboard navigation, tap targets, and color contrast.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:chrome-devtools: Uses Chrome DevTools via MCP for efficient debugging, troubleshooting and browser automation. Use when debugging web pages, automating browser interactions, analyzing performance, or inspecting network requests. This skill does not apply to `--slim` mode (MCP configuration).
- chrome-devtools-mcp:chrome-devtools-cli: Use this skill to write shell scripts or run shell commands to automate tasks in the browser or otherwise use Chrome DevTools via CLI.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:debug-optimize-lcp: Guides debugging and optimizing Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) using Chrome DevTools MCP tools. Use this skill whenever the user asks about LCP performance, slow page loads, Core Web Vitals optimization, or wants to understand why their page's main content takes too long to appear. Also use when the user mentions "largest contentful paint", "page load speed", "CWV", or wants to improve how fast their hero image or main content renders.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:memory-leak-debugging: Diagnoses and resolves memory leaks in JavaScript/Node.js applications. Use when a user reports high memory usage, OOM errors, or wants to analyze heapsnapshots or run memory leak detection tools like memlab.
- chrome-devtools-mcp:troubleshooting: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP and documentation to troubleshoot connection and target issues. Trigger this skill when list_pages, new_page, or navigate_page fail, or when the server initialization fails.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium вАФ an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, вА¶), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system вАФ elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark вАФ using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` вАФ swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior вАФ drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) вАФ there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; highвЖТmax: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only вАФ it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK вАФ model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER вАФ read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" вАФ whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) вАФ never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named вАФ don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Telegram-Kiro-Bridge е∞Иж°И

## ж¶Вињ∞

telegram-kiro-bridge жШѓдЄАеАЛ Telegram Bot вЖФ ACP Agent ж©ЛжО•еЩ®пЉМдљНжЦЉ `G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main`гАВйАПйБО ACP JSON-RPC over stdio жО•дЄК Kiro CLI / Codex / Claude / Gemini з≠Й agentпЉМиЃУдљњзФ®иАЕеЬ®жЙЛж©ЯдЄКзЫіжО•иЈЯ AI agent е∞Ни©±гАБиЈСеЈ•еЕЈгАБзЃ°зРЖйХЈжЬЯи®ШжЖґгАВе∞Иж°ИеП¶еРЂ desktop-pet Electron ж°МйЭҐеѓµзЙ©еКЯиГљгАВ

## AIMemory з≥їзµ±

е∞Иж°ИзЪДйХЈжЬЯи®ШжЖґз≥їзµ±дљНжЦЉ `G:\AI\AIMemory`пЉМеМЕеРЂдї•дЄЛе≠РзµРжІЛпЉЪ

- **facts** вАФ master fact log + topic shards
- **topics** вАФ еИЖй°Юи¶ПеЙЗпЉИ`topics.json`пЉЙ
- **wiki** вАФ зµРжІЛеМЦзЯ•и≠ШеЇЂпЉИconcepts / references / lessons / queriesпЉЙ
- **dailylog** вАФ жѓПжЧ•жСШи¶Б
- **sessions** вАФ е∞Ни©±зіАйМДпЉИиЩХзРЖеЃМжРђеИ∞ oldSessionsпЉЙ

жѓПжЧ•еЗМжЩ® 04:00 зФ± `/dream` иЗ™еЛХзґ≠йБЛпЉИmemorytoskill вЖТ topicreview вЖТ wikisync вЖТ factlint вЖТ wikilint вЖТ skilllint вЖТ specialistreview вЖТ artifactcleanup вЖТ backup вЖТ restartпЉЙгАВ

## жЦЗдїґиИЗжХЩе≠Є

- `docs/usage-guide.html` вАФ еКЯиГљжХЩе≠Єй†БйЭҐпЉМжЈ±иЙ≤дЄїй°МпЉМ24 зЂ†зѓАйЩДзѓДдЊЛпЉИеРЂ Environment PreambleгАБEvent LogгАБAI.mdгАБRTK ShellгАБCodegraphгАБ429 йШ≤и≠ЈпЉЙ
- `docs/llm-to-ai-agent-summary.html` вАФ гАМеЊЮLLMеИ∞AI_Agent.pdfгАНйЗНйїЮе≠ЄзњТжХізРЖй†БйЭҐпЉИжЈ±иЙ≤дЄїй°МгАБ8 зЂ†зѓАгАБзЫЃйМДиЈ≥иљЙпЉЙ
- `docs/hermes-vs-bridge.html` вАФ Hermes AI Agent vs Bridge еКЯиГљжѓФиЉГй†БйЭҐпЉИ7 еНАе°КжѓФиЉГи°® + 6 еЉµи©ХеИЖеН°пЉЙ

dream.json еЈ≤еїЇзЂЛжЦЉ `~/.kiro/dream.json`пЉИ14 ж≠•пЉМеРЂ fork зН®жЬЙзЪД docupdateпЉЙпЉМдЄНеЖНдЊЭи≥і bridge еЕІеїЇ DEFAULT_STEPS fallbackгАВ

### и®≠и®ИеОЯеЙЗ

Bridge жШѓдЄ≠дїЛе±§дЄНжШѓ harnessпЉМдЄНињљж±ВиИЗ Claude Code еКЯиГље∞НйљКпЉЫдњЭжМБеЈЃзХ∞еМЦеД™еЛҐпЉИи™ЮжДПиЈѓзФ± + topic shard + embed-routerпЉЙгАВи©≥и¶Л [[bridge-research]]гАВ

### Git жЮґжІЛ

- upstreamпЉЪ`redkilin/telegram-kiro-bridge`пЉИremote: upstreamпЉЙ
- forkпЉЪ`jiunchiwang/telegram-kiro-bridge`пЉИremote: originпЉЙ

### QUIET_HOURS йЭЬйїШжЩВжЃµ

`QUIET_HOURS=HH-HH` зТ∞еҐГиЃКжХЄпЉМйЭЬйїШжЬЯйЦУжОТз®ЛеїґйБ≤еИ∞зµРжЭЯжЩВеИїжЙН fireпЉИжФѓжПіиЈ®еНИе§ЬпЉЙгАВзЫЃеЙНжЬ™еХЯзФ®пЉМPassive Monitor жФєзФ® cron з≤ЊзҐЇжОІеИґпЉИ2026-07-08 жФєзВЇжѓПжЧ• 2 жђ°пЉЪ12:00гАБ22:00пЉЙгАВ

### Fork Sync з≠ЦзХ•

зФ® mergeпЉИйЭЮ rebaseпЉЙеРИдљµ upstreamпЉМи°Эз™БиІ£ж±ЇеОЯеЙЗпЉЪupstream жЮґжІЛзВЇдЄїгАБжЙЛеЛХдњЭзХЩ fork зН®жЬЙеКЯиГљпЉИ`/reset clean`гАБ`handleDocUpdate`гАБ`specialist-memory`гАБ`reaction_feedback` eventпЉЙгАВ2026-07-03 еЈ≤еРМж≠•иЗ≥ upstream commit 4118f1dпЉИmerge commit d22a568пЉЙпЉМжЦ∞еҐЮеКЯиГљеРЂ ACP model/effort pinгАБorphan turn flusherгАБTelegram rich streamingгАБsession archive LLM summary + trivial carry-forwardгАБbridge config йЫЖдЄ≠еМЦеИ∞ `MEMORY_DIR/config/`гАВ

Fork зЪД DEFAULT_STEPS жѓФ upstream е§ЪдЄАж≠• `docupdate`пЉИеЕ± 14 ж≠•пЉЙгАВ

2026-07-09 upstreamпЉИefab1abпЉЙеЈ≤жКК fork еКЯиГљ port еЫЮдЄКжЄЄвАФвАФдєЛеЊМеРМж≠•и°Эз™БйЭҐе§ІеєЕзЄЃе∞ПгАВ

2026-07-07 еРМж≠• upstream 23 commitsпЉИ`f6341fd`пЉМ12 ж™Фи°Эз™БжЙЛиІ£гАБsmoke еЕ®зґ†пЉЙпЉЪMoA Phase 1-4пЉИblind review / plan mode / debate / review-panel / read-only MCPпЉЙгАБ`/agent` ACP зЖ±еИЗжПЫгАБauto_trigger semantic routingгАВжХЩи®УпЉЪ`git checkout --theirs/--ours` жШѓжХіж™ФеПЦдї£пЉМжЬГжіЧжОЙе∞НеБіеЈ≤дєЊжЈ®иЗ™еЛХеРИдљµзЪД hunkвАФвАФйЫЩйВКйГљжЬЙжФєеЛХзЪДж™Фж°ИжЗЙзФ® `git merge-file` дЄЙжЦєеРИдљµдЄ¶йАРж™Фж†Єе∞НгАВеРМжЧ•еП¶еЃМжИРпЉЪTelegram reply/quote еЉХзФ®ж≥®еЕ• agent promptпЉИ`1346519`пЉЙгАБдњЃ upstream check-moa е£ЮжЄђи©¶пЉИ`e75b45e`пЉМresolvePreset жФє async + embedding routing еПЦдї£ keyword routing еЊМжЄђи©¶жЬ™иЈЯдЄКпЉЙгАВ

### Preamble зШ¶иЇЂпЉИ2026-07-06пЉЙ

18.6k вЖТ ~11.7k chars дЄЙж≠•пЉЪfacts tail 30вЖТ15вЖТ10пЉИ`MEMORY_PREAMBLE_TAIL`пЉЙгАБ7 еАЛ guideline еНАе°КеРИдљµжИРеЦЃдЄА `[Agent disciplines]`гАВеПЦжН®пЉЪдљФ context 5-6% зВЇи≠¶жИТзЈЪпЉЫtail йЭ† topic зіҐеЉХ + embedding еПђеЫЮи£ЬдљНпЉМдљЖдЄНз†НеИ∞ 5 еЫ†зВЇ facts зИЖзЩЉеЉПеѓЂеЕ•жЬГжЦЈиЈ®жЧ•еЈ•дљЬйА£зЇМжАІпЉИembedding жМЙи™ЮжДПдЄНжМЙжЩВйЦУеПђеЫЮи£ЬдЄНдЇЖдљНпЉЙгАВ

2026-07-06 еЖНжђ°еРМж≠• upstream 8 commitsпЉИйЫґи°Эз™БгАБtsc йАЪйБОпЉЙпЉМжЦ∞еҐЮпЉЪ`SPEC-acp-hot-swap.md`пЉИ`/agent` hot-swap и¶Пж†ЉпЉЙгАБ`SPEC-moa-provider.md`пЉИMoA provider еРЂ preset auto-routing + workflowпЉЙгАБusageStore skill-usage.json йЫЖдЄ≠еИ∞ `MEMORY_DIR/config/`гАВеЕ©дїљ SPEC еЭЗзВЇ draftгАБжЬ™еѓ¶дљЬпЉИacp-hot-swap W1-W7гАБmoa-provider W1-W18пЉЙпЉМиИЗ NotebookLM дњЃеЊ©дЄ¶еИЧдЄЙй†ЕжЪЂзЈ©еЊЕиЊ¶гАВ

### Karpathy Wiki е∞НйљК P0пЉИеЃМжИРпЉЙ

дЄЙеАЛж®°зµДеЈ≤еѓ¶дљЬпЉИcommit 6931445пЉМ+1302 и°МпЉЙпЉЪ

3. **query-auto-save.ts**пЉИ230 и°МпЉЙвАФ иЗ™еЛХеБµжЄђеД™и≥™еЫЮи¶Же≠ШзВЇ wiki еАЩйБЄ

жО•зЈЪ bug зФ± Claude Fable 5 дњЃж≠£пЉИcommit d043253 + d3c35c6пЉЙпЉЪtopics.json жђДдљНиІ£жЮРгАБshard ж™ФеРНж≠£и¶ПеМЦгАБfactId ж†ЉеЉПдЄАиЗіжАІгАБrun-prompt / wikisync зФЯзФҐзЂѓжО•зЈЪгАБactivity-log иЈѓеЊСе∞НйљКгАВ

еОЯ P2 еЊЕеБЪпЉМupstream зЪД `archiveSummaryEnabled` + llama.cpp зЬЯжСШи¶БеЈ≤еЃМеЕ®и¶ЖиУЛж≠§йЬАж±ВпЉМдЄНеЖНзН®зЂЛињљиє§гАВ

### еЊЕеБЪжЦєж°ИзЄљи¶ље†±еСК

`docs/pending-roadmap.html`пЉИжЈ±иЙ≤дЄїй°МгАБзЫЃйМДиЈ≥иљЙпЉЙпЉМи®ШйМДжЙАжЬЙжЬ™еЃМжИР roadmap й†ЕзЫЃзЪДзЛАжЕЛиИЗеЙНзљЃжҐЭдїґгАВ

иЃАеЃМжХій†БдЇЖгАВеЃГзЪДеХПй°МдЄНеП™жШѓйХЈпЉМйВДжЬЙзµРжІЛжАІйЫЬи®КпЉЪ**гАМзЫЄйЧЬгАНsection еЗЇзПЊеЕ©жђ°**пЉИ179 и°МеТМ 272 и°МпЉЙпЉМгАМињСжЬЯжФєеЦДпЉИ2026-06-26~28пЉЙгАНжШѓеАЛйЫЬзЙ©е†ЖвАФвАФеЇХдЄЛе°ЮдЇЖ Git жЮґжІЛгАБFork Sync ж≠ЈеП≤гАБPreamble зШ¶иЇЂгАБKarpathy P0 з≠Йж†єжЬђдЄНжШѓгАМињСжЬЯгАНдєЯељЉж≠§зД°йЧЬзЪДжЭ±и•њгАВ

жЛЖеИЖжЦєж°Ие¶ВдЄЛпЉМжѓФзЕІ bridge-acp / bridge-session / bridge-streaming зЪДеЕИдЊЛпЉИжЛЖеЗЇзН®зЂЛй†БпЉМеОЯй†БзХЩ 2-3 и°МжМЗж®Щ stubпЉЙпЉЪ

**жЛЖеЗЇеЕ©еАЛжЦ∞й†БпЉЪ**

1. **`bridge-memory`пЉИи®ШжЖґиИЗзґ≠йБЛпЉЙ**пЉМзіД 90 и°М вАФ жРђиµ∞пЉЪAIMemory з≥їзµ±гАБеВЩдїљж©ЯеИґгАБFactlint дЄЙе±§йШ≤з¶¶гАБTopic еИЖй°Юз≥їзµ±гАБWiki зЯ•и≠ШеЇЂгАБEmbedding RouterпЉИеРЂиІ£иА¶дњЃеЊ©гАБNotebookLM жЗЄж°ИпЉЙгАБDream йЕНзљЃгАВйАЩжШѓжЬАе§ІзЪДдЄїй°МзЊ§пЉМдЄФи™ЮжДПдЄКиЗ™жИРдЄАйЂФгАВ
2. **`bridge-specialist`пЉИSpecialist еИЖиЇЂз≥їзµ±пЉЙ**пЉМзіД 50 и°М вАФ жРђиµ∞пЉЪSpecialist еИЖиЇЂз≥їзµ±гАБToken еЯЈи°МжђКйЩРе±§гАБSpecialist DashboardпЉИеРЂжЮґжІЛйЩЈйШ±гАБеЃЙеЕ®еК†еЫЇпЉЙгАБPARALLEL_DELEGATE cross-checkгАВ

**еРМжЩВеБЪзЪДжЄЕзРЖпЉЪ**

- еИ™жОЙйЗНи§ЗзЪДгАМзЫЄйЧЬгАНsection
- гАМињСжЬЯжФєеЦДгАНжЛЖиІ£пЉЪFork Sync / Git жЮґжІЛж≠ЄеЕ•дЄїй†БгАМйГ®зљ≤иИЗ GitгАНжЃµпЉИж≠ЈеП≤зі∞зѓАе£УзЄЃеИ∞ 5 и°МеЕІпЉЙпЉЫOptimistic CancelгАБ/skillsearchгАБUI дњЃеЊ©з≠ЙдЄАжђ°жАІзіАйМДе£УжИРжҐЭеИЧпЉЫKarpathy P0 еЈ≤еЃМжИРзЪДеѓ¶дљЬзі∞зѓАз≤Њз∞°пЉИroadmap html жЬЙеЃМжХізЙИпЉЙ
- дЄїй†БжЬАзµВзіД **100~110 и°М**пЉЪж¶Вињ∞гАБжЦЗдїґгАБйГ®зљ≤/GitгАБи®КжБѓжОТзЙИгАБеУБи≥™ж©ЯеИґгАБHeadroom и©ХдЉ∞гАБеЊЕеБЪзЄљи¶љгАБеК†дЄК 5 еАЛе≠Рй†БжМЗж®Щ

**йЕНе•ЧеЛХдљЬ**пЉИиЃУжЛЖеИЖйХЈжХИиАМдЄНжШѓдЄАжђ°жАІпЉЙпЉЪ`topics.json` еК†е∞НжЗЙ keyword и¶ПеЙЗпЉМиЃУжЬ™дЊЖ specialist / memory зЫЄйЧЬ facts зЫіжО•еИЖжµБеИ∞жЦ∞ shardпЉМwikisync жЙНжЬГжМБзЇМжЫіжЦ∞жЦ∞й†БиАМдЄНжШѓзєЉзЇМзБМеЫЮ bridge-projectгАВpreamble зЪД wiki зіҐеЉХжШѓеЛХжЕЛзФЯжИРзЪДпЉМжЦ∞й†БжЬГиЗ™еЛХеЗЇзПЊпЉМдЄНзФ®жФє codeгАВ

---
title: Bridge и®ШжЖґиИЗзґ≠йБЛз≥їзµ±

updated: 2026-07-11

# Bridge и®ШжЖґиИЗзґ≠йБЛз≥їзµ±

> 2026-07-11 еЊЮ [[bridge-project]] жЛЖеЗЇгАВжґµиУЛ AIMemory зµРжІЛгАБ/dream зґ≠йБЛгАБfactlintгАБtopic еИЖй°ЮгАБwiki зЯ•и≠ШеЇЂгАБembedding routerгАБеВЩдїљгАВ

## AIMemory зµРжІЛ

йХЈжЬЯи®ШжЖґз≥їзµ±дљНжЦЉ `G:\AI\AIMemory`пЉЪ

- **facts** вАФ master fact logпЉИ`facts-<userId>.md`пЉЙ+ topic shardsпЉИ`facts_Topic/<userId>/<topic>.md`пЉЙ
- **topics** вАФ еИЖй°Юи¶ПеЙЗпЉИ`topics.json`пЉЙ
- **wiki** вАФ зµРжІЛеМЦзЯ•и≠ШеЇЂпЉИconcepts / references / lessons / queriesпЉЙ
- **dailylog** вАФ жѓПжЧ•жСШи¶Б
- **sessions** вАФ е∞Ни©±зіАйМДпЉИиЩХзРЖеЃМжРђеИ∞ oldSessionsпЉЙ
- **config** вАФ bridge йЫЖдЄ≠еМЦйЕНзљЃпЉИacp-providersгАБskill-usage з≠ЙпЉЙ

## /dream е§ЬйЦУзґ≠йБЛ

жѓПжЧ•еЗМжЩ® 04:00 иЗ™еЛХеЯЈи°М 14 ж≠•пЉЪmemorytoskill вЖТ topicreview вЖТ wikisync вЖТ factlint вЖТ wikilint вЖТ skilllint вЖТ specialistreview вЖТ artifactcleanup вЖТ docupdate вЖТ backup вЖТ restart з≠ЙгАВ`dream.json` дљНжЦЉ `~/.kiro/dream.json`пЉИfork жѓФ upstream е§Ъ docupdate дЄАж≠•пЉЙпЉМдЄНдЊЭи≥і bridge еЕІеїЇ DEFAULT_STEPS fallbackгАВзґ≠йБЛзµРжЮЬеѓЂ `STATE.md`пЉИдЄЙе±§пЉЪHigh Priority / Watch List / NoiseпЉЙгАВ

## Topic еИЖй°Юз≥їзµ±

`topics.json` еЃЪзЊ© keyword-based first-match-wins еИЖй°Юи¶ПеЙЗпЉМbridge жѓП 2 зІТйЗНиЃАпЉИжФєеЃМеН≥зФЯжХИпЉМдЄНйЬАйЗНеХЯпЉЙгАВfact жЦЗе≠ЧиљЙе∞ПеѓЂеЊМеБЪ substring жѓФе∞НпЉМзД°еМєйЕНж≠Є miscгАВtopicreview ж≠•й©ЯжЬГеЃЪжЬЯеѓ¶йЂФйЗНеИЖ shardпЉИе¶В 2026-07-10 жЦ∞еїЇ bridge-streaming shardгАБmisc жЄЕйЫґпЉЙгАВ

## Wiki зЯ•и≠ШеЇЂ

wikisync ж≠•й©ЯжКК topic shard иТЄй§ЊжИР wiki й†БпЉМйЦАж™ї вЙ•5 facts иЗ™еЛХзФҐеЗЇ concepts й†БгАВйЕНе•Чж®°зµДпЉИKarpathy P0пЉМcommit 6931445пЉЙпЉЪ

# Bridge Specialist еИЖиЇЂз≥їзµ±

> 2026-07-11 еЊЮ [[bridge-project]] жЛЖеЗЇгАВжґµиУЛ specialist йЕНзљЃгАБtoken еЯЈи°МжђКйЩРе±§гАБзЫ£жОІ DashboardгАБдЄ¶и°МеІФжіЊеУБи≥™ж©ЯеИґгАВ

## еИЖиЇЂйЕНзљЃ

`specialist-domains.json` йЕНзљЃ 3 еАЛеИЖиЇЂпЉИ2026-06-24 еїЇзЂЛпЉМmodel 2026-07-01 жЫіжЦ∞пЉЙпЉЪ

- **slot-dev**пЉЪUK иАБиЩОж©ЯйЦЛзЩЉпЉИclaude-sonnet-4.6пЉМmemory MCPпЉЙ

- **general**пЉЪеЃМжХіиГљеКЫдЄ¶и°Ме§ЪеЈ•пЉИinheritsAllпЉМclaude-sonnet-4.6пЉМmemory + google MCPпЉЙ

и®≠и®Иж±Їз≠ЦпЉЪдЄНеїЇ bridge-dev specialistвАФвАФдЄї agent еЈ•дљЬзЫЃйМДе∞±жШѓ bridge repoпЉМbridge-dev жШѓйЩНзіЪеЖЧй§ШгАВ

дЇТеЛХж®°еЉПеЕ©з®ЃпЉЪ`SPECIALIST_PROXY`пЉИеН≥жЩВе∞Ни©±пЉМдљњзФ®иАЕи®КжБѓзЫійАБ specialist зЫіеИ∞ /backпЉЙгАБ`PARALLEL_DELEGATE`пЉИиГМжЩѓдЄ¶и°МдїїеЛЩпЉМзµРжЮЬдЄАжђ°ж≥®еЕ•дЄї sessionпЉЙгАВж≠ЈеП≤зФҐеЗЇе≠Ш `${MEMORY_DIR}/artifacts/`пЉИзµРжІЛеМЦ JSONпЉЙгАВ

## Token еЯЈи°МжђКйЩРе±§пЉИ2026-07-07пЉМcommit 028a5eaпЉЙ

- `src/token-policy.ts` зЪД `TOKEN_POLICY` й°ѓеЉПзЩљеРНеЦЃпЉЪmain еЕ®йЦЛ / proxy йЩР sendFiles¬Јask¬ЈskillUsages¬Јsticker¬ЈrememberFacts / delegate еЕ®з¶БпЉЫ`isTokenAllowed()` enforcement
- Specialist memory еЫЮеѓЂйЩД `[via specialist:<name>]` provenanceгАБеЦЃжђ°дЄКйЩР 5 жҐЭ
- и©ХдЉ∞зµРиЂЦпЉЪbridge еОЯжЬђе∞±жЬЙйЪ±жАІ gateпЉМй°ѓеЉПеМЦжШѓйШ≤жЬ™дЊЖ refactor и™§йЦЛпЉЫеФѓдЄАеѓ¶йЪЫйҐ®йЪ™жШѓе§ЦйГ®еЕІеЃєвЖТspecialistвЖТfacts зЪД**и®ШжЖґж±°жЯУ**иЈѓеЊСпЉМеЈ≤зФ± provenance + дЄКйЩРзЈ©иІ£
- еИЖе±§жђКйЩР preamble и©ХдЉ∞еЊМдЄНеБЪпЉИеГЕ cosmeticпЉМspecialist preamble еЈ≤жЬЙ scope еИЖе±§пЉЙ

## еУБи≥™ж©ЯеИґпЉЪPARALLEL_DELEGATE cross-check

вЙ•2 specialist зµРжЮЬжЩВиЗ™еЛХж≥®еЕ•дЇ§еПЙй©Чи≠ЙжМЗеЉХпЉМеАЯйП° Claude Code Dynamic Workflows зЪД adversarial review ж¶ВењµгАВи®≠и®Иж±Їз≠ЦпЉЪеП™еАЯйП° cross-check patternпЉМдЄНжРђеЛХжЕЛ delegation plan еТМ script жМБдєЕеМЦпЉИжЮґжІЛеЃЪдљНдЄНеРМгАБи¶Пж®°дЄНйЬАи¶БпЉЙгАВ

## Specialist DashboardпЉИ2026-07-10пЉЙ

Status serverпЉИport 3847пЉЙжУіеЕЕзВЇ specialist зЫ£жОІйЭҐжЭњпЉЪ

- **жКАи°УйБЄеЮЛ**пЉЪе§Ъй†БйЭҐ hash-based SPA + зіФ HTML/vanilla JSпЉИжОТйЩ§ React SPA е§™йЗНгАБжОТйЩ§жЬАе∞ПеҐЮйЗПжУіе±ХжАІеЈЃпЉЙ
- **еЕ•еП£**пЉЪзАПи¶љеЩ®зЫійЦЛ `localhost:3847` + Telegram Mini App жМЙйИХпЉИElectron еЈ≤зІїйЩ§пЉЙ
- **зФ®йАФеЃЪдљН**пЉЪеН≥жЩВзЫ£жОІпЉИAпЉЙ+ жЧ•еЄЄзЃ°зРЖпЉИBпЉЙпЉМйЭЮзіФйЩ§йМѓ
- **еЃЙеЕ®еК†еЫЇ**пЉИcommit c9174e3пЉЙпЉЪasync йМѓи™§йВКзХМйШ≤ crashгАБй†Ри®≠зґБ `127.0.0.1`пЉИ`STATUS_BIND_HOST` еПѓжФєпЉЙгАБзІїйЩ§ CORS `*`гАБenv ж©ЯеѓЖйБЃзљ©гАБpreamble иЈѓзФ± specialist зЩљеРНеЦЃгАБartifact ж™ФеРН specialist name йМ®еЃЪ regex

### жЮґжІЛйЩЈйШ±

- `index.ts` еЕ®еЯЯ `unhandledRejection` handler жЬГ `process.exit(1)`вАФвАФдїїдљХеРМ process зЪД async callback жЬ™жНХжНЙ throw йГљжЬГжЃЇжОЙжХіеАЛ bridgeпЉМжЦ∞еҐЮ server/handler ењЕй†ИиЗ™еЄґйМѓи™§йВКзХМ

## зЫЄйЧЬ

# Bridge Specialist еИЖиЇЂз≥їзµ±

> 2026-07-11 еЊЮ [[bridge-project]] жЛЖеЗЇгАВжґµиУЛ specialist йЕНзљЃгАБtoken еЯЈи°МжђКйЩРе±§гАБзЫ£жОІ DashboardгАБдЄ¶и°МеІФжіЊеУБи≥™ж©ЯеИґгАВ

## еИЖиЇЂйЕНзљЃ

`specialist-domains.json` йЕНзљЃ 3 еАЛеИЖиЇЂпЉИ2026-06-24 еїЇзЂЛпЉМmodel 2026-07-01 жЫіжЦ∞пЉЙпЉЪ

и®≠и®Иж±Їз≠ЦпЉЪдЄНеїЇ bridge-dev specialistвАФвАФдЄї agent еЈ•дљЬзЫЃйМДе∞±жШѓ bridge repoпЉМbridge-dev жШѓйЩНзіЪеЖЧй§ШгАВ

дЇТеЛХж®°еЉПеЕ©з®ЃпЉЪ`SPECIALIST_PROXY`пЉИеН≥жЩВе∞Ни©±пЉМдљњзФ®иАЕи®КжБѓзЫійАБ specialist зЫіеИ∞ /backпЉЙгАБ`PARALLEL_DELEGATE`пЉИиГМжЩѓдЄ¶и°МдїїеЛЩпЉМзµРжЮЬдЄАжђ°ж≥®еЕ•дЄї sessionпЉЙгАВж≠ЈеП≤зФҐеЗЇе≠Ш `${MEMORY_DIR}/artifacts/`пЉИзµРжІЛеМЦ JSONпЉЙгАВ

## Token еЯЈи°МжђКйЩРе±§пЉИ2026-07-07пЉМcommit 028a5eaпЉЙ

- `src/token-policy.ts` зЪД `TOKEN_POLICY` й°ѓеЉПзЩљеРНеЦЃпЉЪmain еЕ®йЦЛ / proxy йЩР sendFiles¬Јask¬ЈskillUsages¬Јsticker¬ЈrememberFacts / delegate еЕ®з¶БпЉЫ`isTokenAllowed()` enforcement
- Specialist memory еЫЮеѓЂйЩД `[via specialist:<name>]` provenanceгАБеЦЃжђ°дЄКйЩР 5 жҐЭ
- и©ХдЉ∞зµРиЂЦпЉЪbridge еОЯжЬђе∞±жЬЙйЪ±жАІ gateпЉМй°ѓеЉПеМЦжШѓйШ≤жЬ™дЊЖ refactor и™§йЦЛпЉЫеФѓдЄАеѓ¶йЪЫйҐ®йЪ™жШѓе§ЦйГ®еЕІеЃєвЖТspecialistвЖТfacts зЪД**и®ШжЖґж±°жЯУ**иЈѓеЊСпЉМеЈ≤зФ± provenance + дЄКйЩРзЈ©иІ£
- еИЖе±§жђКйЩР preamble и©ХдЉ∞еЊМдЄНеБЪпЉИеГЕ cosmeticпЉМspecialist preamble еЈ≤жЬЙ scope еИЖе±§пЉЙ

## еУБи≥™ж©ЯеИґпЉЪPARALLEL_DELEGATE cross-check

вЙ•2 specialist зµРжЮЬжЩВиЗ™еЛХж≥®еЕ•дЇ§еПЙй©Чи≠ЙжМЗеЉХпЉМеАЯйП° Claude Code Dynamic Workflows зЪД adversarial review ж¶ВењµгАВи®≠и®Иж±Їз≠ЦпЉЪеП™еАЯйП° cross-check patternпЉМдЄНжРђеЛХжЕЛ delegation plan еТМ script жМБдєЕеМЦпЉИжЮґжІЛеЃЪдљНдЄНеРМгАБи¶Пж®°дЄНйЬАи¶БпЉЙгАВ

## Specialist DashboardпЉИ2026-07-10пЉЙ

Status serverпЉИport 3847пЉЙжУіеЕЕзВЇ specialist зЫ£жОІйЭҐжЭњпЉЪ

- **еЕ•еП£**пЉЪзАПи¶љеЩ®зЫійЦЛ `localhost:3847` + Telegram Mini App жМЙйИХпЉИElectron еЈ≤зІїйЩ§пЉЙ
- **зФ®йАФеЃЪдљН**пЉЪеН≥жЩВзЫ£жОІпЉИAпЉЙ+ жЧ•еЄЄзЃ°зРЖпЉИBпЉЙпЉМйЭЮзіФйЩ§йМѓ
- **еЃЙеЕ®еК†еЫЇ**пЉИcommit c9174e3пЉЙпЉЪasync йМѓи™§йВКзХМйШ≤ crashгАБй†Ри®≠зґБ `127.0.0.1`пЉИ`STATUS_BIND_HOST` еПѓжФєпЉЙгАБзІїйЩ§ CORS `*`гАБenv ж©ЯеѓЖйБЃзљ©гАБpreamble иЈѓзФ± specialist зЩљеРНеЦЃгАБartifact ж™ФеРН specialist name йМ®еЃЪ regex

### жЮґжІЛйЩЈйШ±

- `index.ts` еЕ®еЯЯ `unhandledRejection` handler жЬГ `process.exit(1)`вАФвАФдїїдљХеРМ process зЪД async callback жЬ™жНХжНЙ throw йГљжЬГжЃЇжОЙжХіеАЛ bridgeпЉМжЦ∞еҐЮ server/handler ењЕй†ИиЗ™еЄґйМѓи™§йВКзХМ
- AIMemory artifacts ж™ФеРН `<date>_<taskId>_<specialist>_<slug>.json` дЄ≠ taskId еПѓеРЂеЇХзЈЪпЉМpositional `split('_')` иІ£жЮРењЕйМѓпЉМй†ИзФ®еЈ≤зЯ•жђДдљНпЉИspecialist nameпЉЙйМ®еЃЪ regex

## зЫЄйЧЬ

- [[bridge-project]] вАФ е∞Иж°ИзЄљи¶љ

# Telegram-Kiro-Bridge е∞Иж°И

## ж¶Вињ∞

telegram-kiro-bridge жШѓдЄАеАЛ Telegram Bot вЖФ ACP Agent ж©ЛжО•еЩ®пЉМдљНжЦЉ `G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main`гАВйАПйБО ACP JSON-RPC over stdio жО•дЄК Kiro CLI / Codex / Claude / Gemini з≠Й agentпЉМиЃУдљњзФ®иАЕеЬ®жЙЛж©ЯдЄКзЫіжО•иЈЯ AI agent е∞Ни©±гАБиЈСеЈ•еЕЈгАБзЃ°зРЖйХЈжЬЯи®ШжЖґгАВе∞Иж°ИеП¶еРЂ desktop-pet Electron ж°МйЭҐеѓµзЙ©еКЯиГљгАВ

## е≠Рз≥їзµ±зіҐеЉХпЉИеЈ≤жЛЖеИЖй†БпЉЙ

- [[bridge-memory]] вАФ AIMemory зµРжІЛгАБ/dream зґ≠йБЛгАБfactlint дЄЙе±§йШ≤з¶¶гАБtopic еИЖй°ЮгАБwiki зЯ•и≠ШеЇЂгАБembedding routerгАБеВЩдїљ
- [[bridge-specialist]] вАФ Specialist еИЖиЇЂйЕНзљЃгАБtoken еЯЈи°МжђКйЩРе±§гАБзЫ£жОІ DashboardгАБPARALLEL_DELEGATE cross-check
- [[bridge-session]] вАФ Session ж≠Єж™ФиИЗжБҐеЊ©пЉИarchive иТЄй§Ње±§гАБACP resumeгАБ/session е§Ъ sessionгАБtranscript иЈѓеЊСпЉЙ
- [[bridge-acp]] вАФ ACP adapter еИЗжПЫгАБmodel pinгАБharness hooksгАВзЫЃеЙНиµ∞ `claude-agent-acp` + pin `claude-fable-5`пЉИ2026-07-06 иµЈпЉЙ
- [[bridge-streaming]] вАФ Draft API дЄЙйЪОжЃµ lifecycleгАБ4096 жИ™жЦЈгАБ429 йЩРжµБгАБRich Messages
- [[bridge-research]] вАФ з†Фз©ґжЦєеРСиИЗе§ЦйГ®еАЯйП°гАБroadmap

## жЦЗдїґиИЗжХЩе≠Є

- `docs/usage-guide.html` вАФ еКЯиГљжХЩе≠Єй†БйЭҐпЉМжЈ±иЙ≤дЄїй°МпЉМ24 зЂ†зѓАйЩДзѓДдЊЛ
- `docs/pending-roadmap.html` вАФ еЊЕеБЪжЦєж°ИзЄљи¶љпЉИжЈ±иЙ≤дЄїй°МгАБзЫЃйМДиЈ≥иљЙпЉЙпЉМи®ШйМДжЙАжЬЙжЬ™еЃМжИР roadmap й†ЕзЫЃ
- `docs/llm-to-ai-agent-summary.html`гАБ`docs/hermes-vs-bridge.html`гАБ`docs/karpathy-wiki-alignment-roadmap.html` вАФ е≠ЄзњТ/жѓФиЉГ/roadmap е∞Ий†Б
- `docs/session-archive-explained.html` вАФ session ж≠Єж™Фи™™жШО

## йГ®зљ≤иИЗ Git

- `start.bat` йЦЛж©ЯиЗ™еЛХеХЯеЛХпЉИ`shell:startup`пЉЙпЉМprocess йААеЗЇеЊМ loop 3 зІТиЗ™еЛХйЗНдЊЖ
- е§Ъж©ЯеЩ®йГ®зљ≤пЉЪжЬђж©Я G: з£БзҐЯпЉИ`MEMORY_DIR=G:\AI\AIMemory`пЉЙпЉМеОЯйЦЛзЩЉж©Я F: з£БзҐЯпЉЫ`.env` ењЕй†Иж≠£зҐЇи®≠ `MEMORY_DIR`гАБ`BACKUP_REPO_DIR`пЉМеР¶еЙЗ /dream еЕ®йГ®е§±жХЧ
- GitпЉЪupstream `redkilin/telegram-kiro-bridge`гАБfork `jiunchiwang/telegram-kiro-bridge`пЉИoriginпЉЙ
- Fork sync з≠ЦзХ•пЉЪmergeпЉИйЭЮ rebaseпЉЙпЉМupstream жЮґжІЛзВЇдЄїгАБжЙЛеЛХдњЭзХЩ fork зН®жЬЙеКЯиГљгАВ2026-07-09 upstream еЈ≤жКК fork еКЯиГљ port еЫЮдЄКжЄЄпЉМдєЛеЊМи°Эз™БйЭҐе§ІеєЕзЄЃе∞П
- еРМж≠•жХЩи®УпЉЪ`git checkout --theirs/--ours` жШѓжХіж™ФеПЦдї£пЉМжЬГжіЧжОЙе∞НеБіеЈ≤дєЊжЈ®иЗ™еЛХеРИдљµзЪД hunkвАФвАФйЫЩйВКйГљжЬЙжФєеЛХзЪДж™Фж°ИжЗЙзФ® `git merge-file` дЄЙжЦєеРИдљµдЄ¶йАРж™Фж†Єе∞Н
- еЕ©дїљ upstream SPEC зВЇ draft жЬ™еѓ¶дљЬпЉИacp-hot-swapгАБmoa-providerпЉЙпЉМиИЗ NotebookLM дњЃеЊ©дЄ¶еИЧжЪЂзЈ©еЊЕиЊ¶

## и®КжБѓжОТзЙИ

Telegram и®КжБѓзФ® HTML parse_modeпЉИ`src/format-html.ts`пЉМMarkdown вЖТ Telegram HTMLпЉЙгАВйБЄ HTML иАМйЭЮ MarkdownV2пЉЪagent иЉЄеЗЇеЄЄеРЂ `_ * [ ]`пЉМMarkdownV2 иЈ≥иДЂе§™еЪіжЬГе§ІйЗП 400 errorпЉЫHTML еП™йЬА escape `<>&`гАВжѓПеАЛ editMessageText йГљжЬЙ strip-tags fallbackгАВйАБ `.md` ж™ФжФєзФ® `.txt` й°ѓз§ЇеРНиІ£ж±Ї in-app viewer дЄ≠жЦЗдЇВзҐЉгАВRich Messages / draft еМЦзі∞зѓАи¶Л [[bridge-streaming]]гАВ

## Preamble иИЗ Steering

- **User Profile зµРжІЛеМЦж≥®еЕ•**пЉИ2026-07-02пЉЙпЉЪ`${MEMORY_DIR}/user-profile.md` зН®зЂЛе≠ШжФЊдљњзФ®иАЕзХЂеГПпЉИ5 еНАе°КпЉЙпЉМpreamble еЫЇеЃЪж≥®еЕ•гАВзН®зЂЛжИРж™ФжШѓеЫ†зВЇзХЂеГПжШѓз©©еЃЪзµРжІЛеМЦи≥ЗжЦЩпЉМжЈЈеЬ® facts и™ЮжДПеПђеЫЮдЄНдњЭи≠ЙжѓПжђ°ж≥®еЕ•
- **Preamble зШ¶иЇЂ**пЉИ2026-07-06пЉЙпЉЪ18.6k вЖТ ~11.7k charsпЉИfacts tail 30вЖТ10гАБ7 еАЛ guideline еНАе°КеРИдљµжИР `[Agent disciplines]`пЉЙгАВи≠¶жИТзЈЪпЉЪдљФ context 5-6%пЉЫtail дЄНз†НеИ∞ 5 еЫ†зВЇ facts зИЖзЩЉеЉПеѓЂеЕ•жЬГжЦЈиЈ®жЧ•йА£зЇМжАІ
- **Steering еК†еЉЈ**пЉЪContext Budget DisciplineпЉИдЇЛеЙНдЉ∞зЃЧ + 70% зЖФжЦЈи≠¶еСКпЉЙгАБASK Button DisciplineпЉИ2+ йБЄй†ЕеЉЈеИґ `<<ASK:...>>`пЉЙ
- **Reply/Quote Context**пЉИ2026-07-07пЉМcommit 1346519пЉЙпЉЪиЃА reply_to_message иИЗ partial quoteпЉМзµД `[Reply context]` еЙНзљЃжЦЉ agent promptпЉМжИ™ 500 е≠Ч

## еЕґдїЦеКЯиГљзіАйМД

- **Optimistic Cancel**пЉЪ/cancel зЂЛеН≥жЄЕ inflight + еБЬ streaming + й°ѓз§ЇгАМвЫФ еЈ≤еПЦжґИгАНпЉМforce-kill timeout 60sвЖТ15s
- **/skillsearch**пЉЪSkillsMP API жРЬе∞ЛеЕђйЦЛ SKILL.mdпЉМеЃЙи£ЭеЊМиЗ™еЛХиЈС sync.ps1
- **/intel жГЕе†±жОТз®Л**пЉЪai + game-industry жѓПжЧ• 08:00гАБtopic-ai podcast йЪФе§© 08:00пЉИsplit з≠ЦзХ•йБњеЕНжЧ©жЩ®и≥Зи®КйБОиЉЙпЉЙ
- **QUIET_HOURS**пЉЪйЭЬйїШжЩВжЃµжОТз®ЛеїґйБ≤пЉМзЫЃеЙНжЬ™еХЯзФ®пЉЫPassive Monitor жФє cron жѓПжЧ• 2 жђ°пЉИ12:00гАБ22:00пЉЙ
- **UI дњЃеЊ©**пЉЪ/help keyboard parse_mode жФє HTML + escHtmlпЉЫгАМињФеЫЮйБЄеЦЃгАНcallback data жФє `help:_back` йБњеЕНжТЮеРН

## еЈ≤зЯ•йЩЈйШ±

- Smoke test зТ∞еҐГйЪФйЫҐпЉЪbridge session еЕІиЈС `scripts/check-*.mjs` жЬГеБЗе§±жХЧпЉИзєЉжЙњз©Ї env иУЛжОЙ dotenvпЉЙпЉМиЈСеЙН `env -u`пЉИдњЭзХЩ MEMORY_DIRпЉЙ
- `check-preamble.mjs`пЉЪfacts зВЇз©ЇжЩВ memory block header дЄНжЄ≤жЯУпЉМfixed core и®ИзЃЧжЬГжґµиУЛжХідїљ preamble
- `RELAY_DELEGATE` tool note еП™еЬ® `config.relay` йЦЛеХЯдЄФ `relay-peers.json` е≠ШеЬ®жЩВж≥®еЕ•пЉИзЫЃеЙНзФЯзФҐдЄНеРЂпЉЙ

## Context е£УзЄЃпЉИHeadroom и©ХдЉ∞пЉЙ

жХіеРИеД™еЕИзіЪпЉЪжЦєж°И AпЉИMCP serverпЉМйЫґжФє codeпЉЙ> жЦєж°И DпЉИheadroom learn зН®зЂЛиЈСпЉЙ> жЦєж°И CпЉИlibrary жХіеРИпЉМжЬАжЬЙжХИдљЖжФє coreпЉЙпЉЫжЦєж°И BпЉИproxyпЉЙжОТйЩ§еЫ† Kiro CLI дЄНеРГ `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`гАВ

## и®≠и®ИеОЯеЙЗ

# Telegram-Kiro-Bridge е∞Иж°И

## ж¶Вињ∞

telegram-kiro-bridge жШѓдЄАеАЛ Telegram Bot вЖФ ACP Agent ж©ЛжО•еЩ®пЉМдљНжЦЉ `G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main`гАВйАПйБО ACP JSON-RPC over stdio жО•дЄК Kiro CLI / Codex / Claude / Gemini з≠Й agentпЉМиЃУдљњзФ®иАЕеЬ®жЙЛж©ЯдЄКзЫіжО•иЈЯ AI agent е∞Ни©±гАБиЈСеЈ•еЕЈгАБзЃ°зРЖйХЈжЬЯи®ШжЖґгАВе∞Иж°ИеП¶еРЂ desktop-pet Electron ж°МйЭҐеѓµзЙ©еКЯиГљгАВ

## е≠Рз≥їзµ±зіҐеЉХпЉИеЈ≤жЛЖеИЖй†БпЉЙ

## жЦЗдїґиИЗжХЩе≠Є

- `docs/session-archive-explained.html` вАФ session ж≠Єж™Фи™™жШО

## йГ®зљ≤иИЗ Git

- `start.bat` йЦЛж©ЯиЗ™еЛХеХЯеЛХпЉИ`shell:startup`пЉЙпЉМprocess йААеЗЇеЊМ loop 3 зІТиЗ™еЛХйЗНдЊЖ
- е§Ъж©ЯеЩ®йГ®зљ≤пЉЪжЬђж©Я G: з£БзҐЯпЉИ`MEMORY_DIR=G:\AI\AIMemory`пЉЙпЉМеОЯйЦЛзЩЉж©Я F: з£БзҐЯпЉЫ`.env` ењЕй†Иж≠£зҐЇи®≠ `MEMORY_DIR`гАБ`BACKUP_REPO_DIR`пЉМеР¶еЙЗ /dream еЕ®йГ®е§±жХЧ
- GitпЉЪupstream `redkilin/telegram-kiro-bridge`гАБfork `jiunchiwang/telegram-kiro-bridge`пЉИoriginпЉЙ
- Fork sync з≠ЦзХ•пЉЪmergeпЉИйЭЮ rebaseпЉЙпЉМupstream жЮґжІЛзВЇдЄїгАБжЙЛеЛХдњЭзХЩ fork зН®жЬЙеКЯиГљгАВ2026-07-09 upstream еЈ≤жКК fork еКЯиГљ port еЫЮдЄКжЄЄпЉМдєЛеЊМи°Эз™БйЭҐе§ІеєЕзЄЃе∞П
- еРМж≠•жХЩи®УпЉЪ`git checkout --theirs/--ours` жШѓжХіж™ФеПЦдї£пЉМжЬГжіЧжОЙе∞НеБіеЈ≤дєЊжЈ®иЗ™еЛХеРИдљµзЪД hunkвАФвАФйЫЩйВКйГљжЬЙжФєеЛХзЪДж™Фж°ИжЗЙзФ® `git merge-file` дЄЙжЦєеРИдљµдЄ¶йАРж™Фж†Єе∞Н

## и®КжБѓжОТзЙИ

## Preamble иИЗ Steering

- **Reply/Quote Context**пЉИ2026-07-07пЉМcommit 1346519пЉЙпЉЪиЃА reply_to_message иИЗ partial quoteпЉМзµД `[Reply context]` еЙНзљЃжЦЉ agent promptпЉМжИ™ 500 е≠Ч

## еЕґдїЦеКЯиГљзіАйМД

- **Optimistic Cancel**пЉЪ/cancel зЂЛеН≥жЄЕ inflight + еБЬ streaming + й°ѓз§ЇгАМвЫФ еЈ≤еПЦжґИгАНпЉМforce-kill timeout 60sвЖТ15s
